meaning: n.<日> (第二次世界大战期间日本空军敢死队)神风对队员,此种战术之飞机或驾驶员
examples: As a response to Japanese kamikaze attacks, Grumman developed the F8F Bearcat as a lightweight fighter able to climb quickly to meet incoming enemy aircraft.
Meanwhile, the kamikaze continues to blow.
Suddenly, a word flashes through my mind: Kamikaze.
By all accounts, the Japanese Kamikaze pilots in WWII were earnest, dedicated young men.
If the Papandreou government continues on its austerity kamikaze mission, it will eventually fall.
